novel_features.name: Batch IMO lookup with throttled persistence
novel_features.description: Resolve a list of IMOs in one invocation, honoring the 1 req/2-3s throttle and persisting each to the local cache.
novel_features.rationale: GISIS has no bulk-fetch endpoint; only this CLI orchestrates per-IMO calls under throttle and persists to SQLite for the orchestrator's nightly batch tests.
novel_features.why_it_matters: When an agent needs particulars for many vessels, this is the polite + persistent path. Single IMO? Use 'ship get'.

novel_features.name: Local cache browse with FTS
novel_features.description: Browse vessels you have already fetched, with filters on flag/owner/type and full-text search on name/owner.
novel_features.rationale: GISIS has no 'my recent lookups' view; the local cache becomes a personal vessel index.
novel_features.why_it_matters: When you want to find a vessel you saw before, this beats re-fetching from GISIS.

novel_features.name: Watchlist with selective refresh
novel_features.description: Pin vessels for an active deal or story, then refresh only the pinned ones at a chosen cadence.
novel_features.rationale: GISIS has no per-user watchlist; pinning + scoped re-fetch replaces sync semantics for a lookup-only API.
novel_features.example: gisis-pp-cli ship pin 9966233 --label deal-2026-Q2 && gisis-pp-cli ship refresh --pinned --older-than 30d
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this to keep a working set of vessels current without re-fetching the whole cache.

novel_features.name: Cross-snapshot ship history (flag-hop detector)
novel_features.description: Show how a vessel's particulars have changed across snapshots — flag, name, owner, operator, classification society, status.
novel_features.rationale: GISIS shows current state only; we accumulate immutable snapshots on every fetch and surface per-field diffs with dates.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Flag-hopping and ownership changes are the textbook sanctions-bypass tells in maritime DD. Use this when you need temporal context, not a snapshot.

novel_features.name: Stale-cache report
novel_features.description: List cached vessels whose particulars haven't been refreshed in N days.
novel_features.rationale: Makes lookup-only API's staleness visible and suggests the next refresh batch.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Compliance recency: when you need to know which dossier vessels need re-checking.

novel_features.name: Owner-fleet listing from accumulated cache
novel_features.description: List every cached vessel for a given owner string (the Companies module isn't in v1).
novel_features.rationale: Companies module is deferred; this synthesizes 'ships by owner' from accumulated ship get results.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Counterparty exposure and related-vessel discovery without hitting the deferred Companies module.

novel_features.name: Session liveness ping
novel_features.description: Single fast GET to /Public/SHIPS/Default.aspx; exits 0 if session is live, non-zero if re-login needed.
novel_features.rationale: ASP.NET session expiry is the brief's defining auth pain; this is the idempotent check users wire into cron/launchd.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Long batch jobs and unattended cron tasks need a cheap way to know if the session is still alive.

narrative.headline: Authoritative IMO ship particulars on the command line, plus a local cache that turns one-shot lookups into a compounding vessel index.
narrative.value_prop: GISIS is the IMO's canonical ship registry, gated by a login + Cloudflare Turnstile and only readable from a server-rendered web app. This CLI scrapes it politely (1 req/2-3s), caches every result to local SQLite, exposes 'ship get' as an MCP tool, and adds maritime-DD-specific commands like 'ship history' for flag-hop detection and 'owner fleet' for counterparty exposure that the GISIS web app itself can't answer.
narrative.auth_narrative: GISIS requires a free IMO Web Accounts login (https://webaccounts.imo.org/Register.aspx?App=GISISPublic) with a Cloudflare Turnstile challenge. Programmatic login is blocked, so this CLI uses the press-auth companion: install it once, run 'press-auth login gisis.imo.org', a controlled Chrome window opens for you to sign in, and your cookies are captured into the macOS keychain. The CLI then reads cookies on demand. Sessions die after ~30 min of inactivity; run 'gisis-pp-cli auth ping' from cron to keep them warm, or re-run 'press-auth login' when the session expires.

quickstart.comment: Health check first — confirms binary works without making network calls.
Capture your GISIS session cookies via a controlled Chrome window.
Verify auth — should report cookies present and session live.
Fetch authoritative ship particulars by IMO number.

troubleshoots.symptom: doctor reports 'session expired' or 'login wall detected'
troubleshoots.fix: Re-run 'press-auth login gisis.imo.org' to capture fresh cookies.
Same as above — session died. Re-run 'press-auth login gisis.imo.org'.

troubleshoots.symptom: press-auth login window times out without capturing
troubleshoots.fix: Add --complete-selector a[href*=Logoff] so press-auth knows when login is done.
Default throttle is 1 req/2-3s per the API's polite-use convention. Increase --delay if GISIS starts rate-limiting.

narrative.when_to_use: Use this CLI when you need authoritative IMO ship particulars — name, flag, type, gross tonnage, registered owner, operator, classification society. The local cache makes it a personal vessel index that compounds over time, and 'ship history' surfaces flag/owner changes that matter for sanctions and due diligence. Pair with GFW (vessel events) and AIS Stream (real-time position) via the Vessel MCP orchestrator for a full DD picture.
anti_triggers: Do not use this CLI for real-time vessel position — use AIS Stream instead.
Do not use this CLI for fishing-activity or port-visit events — use Global Fishing Watch (GFW).
Do not use this CLI for OFAC sanctions screening — use the OpenSanctions or OFAC SDN adapter in the Vessel MCP orchestrator.
Do not use this CLI for ship name search in v1 — defer to v0.2; in the meantime, look up the IMO another way.

recipes.title: Look up an IMO with structured output
recipes.explanation: Returns the four high-gravity fields needed for a KYC entry.

recipes.title: Run a nightly batch over a watchlist
recipes.explanation: Resolves every IMO in the file under the throttle and streams JSON-lines.

recipes.title: Spot a flag change on a pinned vessel
recipes.command: gisis-pp-cli ship refresh --pinned --older-than 7d && gisis-pp-cli ship history 9966233 --json
recipes.explanation: Refresh stale pinned ships, then check the diff on one of them.

recipes.title: Find all cached ships under one owner
recipes.explanation: Synthesizes counterparty exposure from accumulated lookups.

recipes.title: Keep the session alive from cron
recipes.explanation: ASP.NET sessions time out at ~30 min idle; ping every 20 to stay warm.
